title: Active Inference Agent Simulations: Adaptive Advantages of Somatic Markers
description: Computational agent simulations test whether somatic marker integration M̂(c,a) = γ_V·V(c,a) + γ_A·A(c,a) confers measurable adaptive advantages over APGI agents lacking somatic marker function. Agents are evaluated on embodied decision tasks under volatility conditions where rapid threshold adaptation is required. Five sub-predictions are tested: (Pred 2.a) full APGI agents converge within 50–80 trials matching human IGT performance and outperform both GNWT-only and Standard PP agents in cumulative reward — all three pairwise comparisons significant; (Pred 2.b) post-ignition action selection entropy increases versus pre-ignition baseline, with 70–85% of ignition events satisfying Πⁱ·|zⁱ| > Πᵉ·|zᵉ|; (Pred 2.c) somatic marker retrieval M̂ temporally precedes threshold crossing θₜ; (Pred 2.d) β_SM lesion specifically degrades performance more than other single-parameter lesions, with the deficit largest in high-volatility blocks (σ_env = 0.6); and (Pred 2.e) the full APGI generative model achieves lower BIC than Standard PP and GNWT-only when fit to human IGT trial-by-trial choice sequences. Fully validated via APGI_Somatic_Marker_Identifiability.py.
prediction_cluster_label: Adaptive Advantages of Somatic Markers (Simulation + Model Comparison)

sub_predictions.claim: Full APGI agents converge within 50–80 trials matching human IGT performance. APGI > GNWT-only > Standard PP in cumulative reward ranking (all three pairwise comparisons).
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: APGI convergence within 50–80 trials; reward ratio APGI/β_SM-lesion > 1.15; APGI > GNWT-only > Pure PP in cumulative reward (all pairwise permutation p < 0.05). Advantage must emerge within 80 trials not just cumulatively.
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No significant pairwise performance advantage across all volatility conditions; or APGI fails convergence within 80 trials even when cumulative reward advantage present.

falsifying_thresholds.condition_1: No significant pairwise performance advantage across all volatility conditions
falsifying_thresholds.condition_2: APGI fails convergence within 80 trials even when cumulative reward advantage present

sub_predictions.claim: Post-ignition action selection entropy increases vs. pre-ignition baseline; 70–85% of ignition events satisfy Πⁱ·|zⁱ| > Πᵉ·|zᵉ| (high interoceptive component).
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Post-ignition entropy > pre-ignition (Wilcoxon p < 0.05); 70–85% of ignitions satisfy Πⁱ·|zⁱ| > Πᵉ·|zᵉ|.
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Ignition uncorrelated with post-ignition behavioral adaptation; or < 60% of ignition events satisfy Πⁱ·|zⁱ| > Πᵉ·|zᵉ| (interoceptive dominance criterion independently falsified).

falsifying_thresholds.condition: < 60% of ignition events satisfy criterion — independently falsified

sub_predictions.claim: Somatic marker retrieval M̂ temporally precedes threshold crossing θₜ (cross-correlation peak at negative lag).
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Cross-correlation analysis (lag −5 to +5 trials): M̂ leads θₜ crossing by ≥ 1 trial in ≥ 75% of ignition events; Kendall τ > 0.3 at negative lag.
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: M̂ activation simultaneous with or lagging threshold crossing; cross-correlation peak at lag 0 or positive.

falsifying_thresholds.condition: Cross-correlation peak at lag 0 or positive lag

sub_predictions.claim: β_SM lesion specifically degrades performance more than other single-parameter lesions; deficit largest in high-volatility blocks (σ_env = 0.6).
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: β_SM agent reward significantly lower than Πⁱ-constant and α-lesion agents under matched volatility (permutation p < 0.05); β_SM vs. Πⁱ-lesion contrast significant specifically at high volatility σ_env = 0.6.
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: β_SM produces equivalent or lesser deficit compared to Πⁱ-lesion under all volatility conditions; or pathway specificity not demonstrated at high volatility.

confirming_thresholds.specificity_required: β_SM vs. Πⁱ-lesion contrast significant at σ_env = 0.6

falsifying_thresholds.condition_1: β_SM produces equivalent or lesser deficit compared to Πⁱ-lesion under all volatility conditions
falsifying_thresholds.condition_2: No pathway specificity at high volatility (σ_env = 0.6)

sub_predictions.claim: Full APGI generative model achieves lower BIC than Standard PP and GNWT-only when fit to human IGT trial-by-trial choice sequences (model parsimony test, distinct from simulation performance).
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: APGI BIC lower than Standard PP by ΔBIC ≥ 10; APGI BIC lower than GNWT-only by ΔBIC ≥ 10; cross-validated on held-out trials (log-likelihood improvement p < 0.01 via LRT).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: APGI BIC ≥ any alternative model's BIC on pre-registered dataset; or ΔBIC < 3 against GNWT-only (interoceptive and somatic parameters add no explanatory value).

confirming_thresholds.dataset: human IGT trial-by-trial choice sequences (pre-registered dataset)

falsifying_thresholds.condition_1: APGI BIC ≥ any alternative model BIC on pre-registered dataset

validation_notes: Fully validated. The APGI_Somatic_Marker_Identifiability.py script is built, integrated, and passes all benchmarks for two-session parameter estimation, Fisher Information Matrix (FIM) block-diagonal verification (condition number < 100), and pathological free-β collinearity demonstration, achieving r > 0.75 across all simulated subjects. Pred 2.e (BIC model comparison) and three-way agent comparison (Pred 2.a) added to match registry EP-2 requirements.

inclusion_criteria: Synthetic agent: computational simulation only — no human participants
Agent initialised with APGI parameters within physiologically plausible bounds
Environment: stochastic multi-armed bandit with volatility schedule (σ_env ∈ {0.1, 0.3, 0.6})
exclusion_criteria: Simulation runs with numerical instability (S_t > 1e6 or NaN values) excluded
Runs where convergence not achieved within 500 trials excluded from analysis

human_igt_dataset.description: Pre-registered human IGT choice sequence dataset for Pred 2.e BIC model comparison
human_igt_dataset.source: Pre-registered open dataset (Ahn et al. 2014 or equivalent); exact dataset locked in OSF pre-registration

apparatus.agent_framework: Custom APGI active inference agent (src/apgi/core.py + somatic marker extension)
apparatus.environment: Stochastic multi-armed bandit (3 arms) with hidden state volatility; reversal probability ∈ {0.02, 0.05, 0.10} per trial
comparison_conditions: Full APGI agent (with somatic marker M̂, β ∈ [0.3, 0.8])
GNWT-only agent (global workspace ignition, no interoceptive precision weighting)
Standard PP agent (continuous prediction error, no ignition threshold)
Πⁱ-lesion agent (Πⁱ_eff = constant, no metabolic gating)
apparatus.random_seeds: 1000 independent seeds per condition for bootstrapped confidence intervals

target.type: Computational trial: agent selects action from {1, 2, 3}, receives stochastic reward signal

trial_structure.response: Agent selects action via softmax policy over precision-weighted prediction errors

blocks.volatility_schedule: Block-wise: σ_env = [0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 0.3, 0.1]; reversal schedule pseudorandom within block
blocks.high_volatility_block: Block 3 (σ_env = 0.6) is the primary test block for Pred 2.d pathway specificity

somatic_marker_model.V_ca: Anticipated value of action a in context c (learned via temporal difference)
somatic_marker_model.A_ca: Anticipated arousal signal associated with action a in context c (proxy for SCR/heart rate)
somatic_marker_model.identifiability_requirement: Block-diagonal Fisher Information Matrix; M̂ must be orthogonal to raw Πⁱ in fitting procedure — requires APGI_Somatic_Marker_Identifiability.py

models.name: Full APGI
models.description: Full model with somatic marker modulation and ignition threshold

models.name: GNWT-only
models.description: Global workspace ignition without interoceptive precision weighting (β_SM = 0, Πⁱ = 0)

models.name: Standard PP
models.description: Continuous prediction error model without ignition threshold (θₜ → 0)

model_comparison_pred_2e.fitting_method: Maximum likelihood estimation on trial-by-trial choice sequences
model_comparison_pred_2e.model_selection_criterion: Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC); lower is better
model_comparison_pred_2e.cross_validation: 5-fold cross-validation on held-out trials; LRT for nested model comparison

dependent_variables: Cumulative reward per agent type over 500 trials (primary outcome — Pred 2.a)
Convergence trial (first trial where running mean reward exceeds 70% optimal) per agent — Pred 2.a
Pairwise reward comparisons: APGI vs. GNWT-only, APGI vs. Standard PP, GNWT-only vs. Standard PP — Pred 2.a
Action selection entropy post-ignition vs. pre-ignition (bits) — Pred 2.b
Proportion of ignition events satisfying Πⁱ·|zⁱ| > Πᵉ·|zᵉ| — Pred 2.b
Cross-correlation lag: M̂ activation vs. θₜ crossing (lag −5 to +5 trials) — Pred 2.c
Proportion of ignition events where M̂ leads by ≥ 1 trial — Pred 2.c
Reward: β_SM-lesion vs. Πⁱ-lesion vs. α-lesion, per volatility block — Pred 2.d
Reward contrast: β_SM-lesion vs. Πⁱ-lesion specifically at σ_env = 0.6 — Pred 2.d
BIC: Full APGI vs. GNWT-only vs. Standard PP on human IGT dataset — Pred 2.e
ΔBIC: Full APGI vs. each alternative — Pred 2.e
LRT log-likelihood improvement on held-out trials — Pred 2.e
Ignition rate per agent type per volatility condition
Threshold adaptation speed (trials to re-optimise after reversal) per agent type
primary_hypothesis: Full APGI agents converge within 50–80 trials and achieve higher cumulative reward than both GNWT-only and Standard PP agents (all pairwise permutation p < 0.05), with the β_SM lesion deficit largest in high-volatility blocks (σ_env = 0.6), and the full APGI generative model achieving ΔBIC ≥ 10 over GNWT-only when fit to human IGT data.
primary_analysis: Pred 2.a: three-way permutation test (10,000 permutations) on cumulative reward; convergence trial comparison. Pred 2.b: Wilcoxon signed-rank for entropy; proportion test for 70–85% interoceptive dominance. Pred 2.c: cross-correlation analysis (lag −5 to +5). Pred 2.d: permutation test for β_SM-lesion deficit specificity at σ_env = 0.6. Pred 2.e: BIC comparison on human IGT dataset; 5-fold cross-validation LRT.

power_analysis.based_on: Damasio somatic marker advantage estimates; active inference agent benchmarks from Friston et al. (2017)

ethics.exempt_reason: No human participants. All simulation data are synthetic; agents are computational simulations only. Human subjects ethics approval is not required. Human IGT dataset (Pred 2.e) is used from publicly available anonymised repository only.

caveats: Somatic marker model M̂(c,a) = γ_V·V(c,a) + γ_A·A(c,a) is one formalisation among several; Damasio's original account does not specify γ_V and γ_A uniquely. Values used here are motivated by interoceptive salience literature but require empirical calibration.
Agent performance advantages depend on volatility magnitude; effect may not replicate in stationary environments.

Pred 2.a requires a GNWT-only agent implementation that exactly parallels Full APGI except for removal of Πⁱ and β_SM; the architecture difference must be documented precisely to avoid confounding by parameter count.
Pred 2.e (BIC comparison on human IGT data) requires the specific dataset to be pre-registered before analysis; post-hoc dataset selection would not satisfy the registry criterion.
